assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check for deprecated API usage with 'kubectl get events --field-selector reason=FailedAPIVersion' and mentions checking flowschemas and prioritylevelconfigurations for 1.32 changes

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response explicitly checks webhook configurations with 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations' and 'kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations' and mentions checking for failed admissions

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes checking resource quotas, allocated resources on nodes, and provides a specific command to patch deployment resource requests/limits

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check kube-system pods including apiserver, etcd, CNI pods, and CoreDNS, plus general pod status checking

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response includes the exact command 'kubectl get --raw="/metrics" | grep apiserver_request_duration' to monitor API server response times

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes checking service mesh components with 'kubectl get pods -n istio-system' and checks CNI/networking components, though NetworkPolicy is not explicitly mentioned

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes checking events with sorting by timestamp, checking for readiness probe failures with 'kubectl describe pods | grep -A 10 "Readiness"', and continuous event monitoring

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) at all, which could be relevant for post-upgrade performance issues
